title: Son of David, Have Mercy
subtitle: The two blind men cry out, are touched, and see

stillness: Be still, and do not hurry into the time of trial.
Chrysostom says: attend to your prayers with diligence,
and if you have not received, abide, that you may receive.
For God delays His assent to those who beseech Him,
that we may flee continually back to Him,
gospel: And as Jesus passed on from there,
two blind men followed Him,
crying out and saying:
Have mercy on us, Son of David.
And when He had come into the house,
the blind men came to Him.
And Jesus said to them:
Do you believe that I can do this for you?
They said to Him: Yes, Lord.
Then He touched their eyes, saying:
According to your faith be it done unto you.
And their eyes were opened.
And as they went out, behold,
they brought to Him a man who was mute,
and, what is worse, possessed by a demon.
And when the demon was cast out, the mute man spoke.
And the crowds marvelled, saying:
Never has the like appeared in Israel.
But the Pharisees said:
By the prince of demons He casts out demons.
behold: See their longing, both in their crying and in their entreaty.
They did not approach simply, but crying out greatly,
asking for nothing else than mercy.
They cried out, because, to the letter, they could not see.
He did not heal them in the road,
but, since the house was near, He led them there to heal them apart,
that He might teach us to flee the praise of the crowd.
Then, after the confession of faith, He touched their eyes.
O happy touch, upon which so great a power follows!
And at once they were enlightened,
consider: Behold how great is the faith of those who simply believe,
which merits the giving of so many gifts,
and their increase from God,
so that all things are possible to him who believes.
Their confession was added to their inner faith,
that they might be the more worthy to be enlightened.
For with the heart one believes unto justice,
but with the mouth confession is made unto salvation.
And the Lord, fleeing the glory of boasting,
charged them: See that no one know it,
that He might instruct us to flee vainglory.
For the just man, when he is praised to his face, is scourged in his mind.
Yet they could not keep silent about the benefit,
but, not ungrateful for the grace of God,
became His heralds and evangelists.
And consider this mute man, bound by the demon,
who held his tongue and would not let him speak.
First the demon is cast out, and then the other offices of the body follow.
But the envious Pharisees, who could not deny the power,
slandered the work,
for hatred and envy interpret deeds to the worse part,
and pervert all judgements.
prayer: Lord Jesus Christ, light of eternal brightness,
that I may never fall asleep in the death of the soul;
I may see all that ought to be done,
and, helped by that same grace, grow strong to fulfil what I have seen;
and thence may recount Your benefits,
to Your glory and the good of others.
Open also, Lord, my mute mouth,
loosing my sins by the infusion of grace,
and freeing my tongue to Your praises;
that, having received the grace of speech,
I may accuse myself, praise You as God,
build up my neighbour, and proclaim the truth. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Felix tactus, ad quem tanta sequitur virtus!
latinJewel.english: O happy touch, upon which so great a power follows!
